title: System.IO Namespace
ques: Which namespace contains types for reading and writing to files? Import it.

title: Checking File Existence
ques: Write code to check if a file "config.xml" **exists** before trying to open it.
explanation: `File.Exists(path)` returns true if the file exists at the specified path.

title: Writing Text to File
ques: Use `File.WriteAllText` to **write** the string "Hello World" to "test.txt" (overwriting content).
explanation: `File.WriteAllText` creates a new file, writes the contents, and then closes the file. If the file exists, it is overwritten.

title: Reading Text from File
ques: Use `File.ReadAllText` to **read** the entire content of "test.txt" into a string variable.
explanation: `File.ReadAllText` opens a text file, reads all lines of the file, and then closes the file.

title: StreamWriter (Appending)
ques: Use **`StreamWriter`** to **append** a line "New Log Entry" to "log.txt". Use `true` for append mode.
explanation: The second parameter of `StreamWriter` constructor determines whether to append (`true`) or overwrite (`false`).

title: StreamReader (Reading Lines)
ques: Use **`StreamReader`** to read a file "data.txt" line by line inside a `while` loop until null.
explanation: `ReadLine()` returns the next line of characters from the input stream, or `null` if the end of the input stream is reached.

title: Directory Operations
ques: Create a new **Directory** named "Backup" and delete it if it exists.
explanation: `Directory` class provides static methods for creating, moving, and enumerating directories.

title: Path Class
ques: Use `Path.Combine` to safely join directory "C:\Data" and file "file.txt". Why use it instead of string concatenation?
explanation: `Path.Combine` automatically handles directory separators (`\` or `/`) correctly for the underlying operating system.

title: File Info
ques: Use **`FileInfo`** to get the size (length) of "image.png" in bytes.
explanation: `FileInfo` provides instance methods and properties for creating, copying, deleting, moving, and opening files, aiding in gathering metadata.

title: BinaryWriter/Reader
ques: Write an integer `100` and a boolean `true` to a binary file "data.bin" using **`BinaryWriter`**.
explanation: `BinaryWriter` writes primitive types in binary format to a stream.
